question_text: Two sealed containers are identical in size. Container A contains only oxygen gas molecules, and Container B contains only helium gas atoms. Both containers are at the same temperature. Which container has particles moving faster on average, and why?

options.label: A
options.text: Container A (oxygen) because oxygen molecules are larger.

options.label: B
options.text: Container B (helium) because helium atoms are lighter and move faster at the same temperature.

options.label: C
options.text: Both containers have particles moving at exactly the same speed because they are at the same temperature.

options.label: D
options.text: Container A (oxygen) because gas molecules move faster than individual atoms.

explanation: Although both containers are at the same temperature, the lighter helium atoms move faster on average than heavier oxygen molecules. This is because temperature represents average kinetic energy (KE = ½mv²), so lighter particles must move faster to have the same total kinetic energy as heavier ones. This is why lighter gases like helium diffuse faster than heavier gases.
